title: Late-Night Debugging Playbook
evidence: Maps debugging, heads-down work, and late-night phrasing to the same low-distraction profile used for coding sessions.
Best when the listener wants steady concentration rather than emotional peaks or vocal intensity.

title: Spin Class Cardio Guide
evidence: Treats spin, HIIT, and interval language as a high-output workout request even when the word gym never appears.
Prefers tracks with strong motion cues, clear drive, and minimal acoustic softness.

title: After-Work Reset Notes
evidence: Converts recovery language into a gentle evening profile instead of leaving the query as a generic happy request.
Useful when the listener wants to settle, recover, or lower stimulation after a long day.

title: Memory-Lane Scrapbooking Guide
evidence: Links scrapbook and memory-lane language to the same reflective acoustic profile used for journaling.
Best for human, cozy, story-rich listening rather than neutral background music.
